steps.title: Use the given line of best fit
steps.content: The equation of the line of best fit is already given: $y=0.096x-0.488$. The slope is the coefficient of $x$, so the slope is $0.096$.

steps.title: Interpret the slope units
steps.content: The $x$-axis is years since 1940, measured in individual years. The $y$-axis is minimum wage in dollars per hour. Therefore a slope of $0.096$ means the model predicts an increase of $0.096$ dollars per hour for each additional year.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Each year between 1940 and 2010, the average increase in minimum wage was 0.096 dollars. says the average increase was $0.096$ dollars each year. Two other options incorrectly treat the change as per 10 years, and the remaining alternative uses the intercept instead of the slope. Each year between 1940 and 2010, the average increase in minimum wage was 0.096 dollars. is correct.
